programmes.show_description: ...Lads? Cold Feet: Inspired 70s sitcom from the creators of Porridge. Bob is embroiled in preparations for his wedding to Thelma, but Terry's return has caused him to have doubts.
...Lads? Moving On: 1970s sitcom sequel by the writers of Porridge and Auf Wiedersehen Pet. Terry is appalled at the changes in his home town. A job interview proves the final straw.
...Lads? Stranger on a Train: Chucklesome sequel to The Likely Lads. After five years apart, the old pals meet by chance. Terry returns to civilian life, while marriage looms for Bob.
...Lads? Home Is the Hero: Brilliant 'buddy sitcom' from the early 1970s. Terry's return has been less than triumphant - his parents are away, it's cold and there's no food in the house.
...Lads? Cold Feet: Inspired 70s sitcom from the creators of Porridge. Bob is embroiled in preparations for his wedding to Thelma, but Terry's return has caused him to have doubts.
...Lads? Moving On: 1970s sitcom sequel by the writers of Porridge and Auf Wiedersehen Pet. Terry is appalled at the changes in his home town. A job interview proves the final straw.
The Giant Speaker: Exploring a bizarre block in Taiwan resembling a giant speaker, a pioneering venture in Alaska, and an alpine village that masks a military beast.
Morpeth to Amble: Michael Portillo explores the wild and rugged coastline of Northumberland by rail, beginning in Morpeth.
Farne Islands to Berwick-upon-Tweed: Michael Portillo's rail trek along the coast of Northumberland continues towards the Farne Islands.
Fairlie to Port Glasgow: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris.
Oban to Staffa: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotland's west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago.
Shopping from Home.
London's Lost Route to the Seas: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go in search of London's lost canal route from Teddington and all the way to the sea at Littlehampton.
Birmingham to Braunston: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel from Birmingham to Braunston for a festival of canal boats and to unveil a plaque to a late friend and canal campaigner.
Shannon Erne Waterway: Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Ireland and the Shannon Erne Waterway to try some folk singing and Irish dancing before visiting the ruins of Crom Castle.
The Gota Canal, Sweden: Timothy West and Prunella Scales set forth from Gothenburg on the first half of the Gota Canal, one of the longest and most beautiful waterways in the world.

Meat Eaters: Witness elusive animals on film in this stunning series filmed around the world. David Attenborough looks at carnivores, from arctic foxes to the Siberian tiger.
Opportunists: World-spanning docu series about our closest animal relations. Zoological hero David Attenborough looks at omnivores - animals that eat whatever is around at the time.
Return to the Water: David Attenborough gives us more incredible insight into the world of mammals. Here the focus is on animals that swim, such as seals, dolphins and even elephants.
Life in the Trees: David Attenborough's major series about some of the world's oddest animals. Here, a look at tree dwellers - mammals which have adapted to a life at height.

St Andrews to Stonehaven: Michael Portillo visits Leuchars, known nowadays on the railways as Leuchars for St Andrews, which is Scotland's oldest university.
Shopping from Home.
Which is faster in a race - a kid on a skateboard or a turbocharged rally car? There's only one way to find out! The Ford Focus is reviewed, while Geri Halliwell is the star guest.
Motoring news and views with those car-crazed chums. Jeremy tests out the Dodge Viper and Richard tries out some incredible prototypes. Joanna Lumley is their fabulous guest.
There's a face-off between the Aston Martin Vanquish S and Ferrari 575, the Smart ForFour is put through its paces and the aptly named Jimmy Carr hits the test track.

The Sporting Life: Classic laughs from the Medfords. The vicar enlists Terry's help to assemble a ping-pong table for the youth club. An easy task? Not for Terry!
The Dish: Suburban sitcom featuring the talents of Terry Scott and June Whitfield. A jealous Terry discovers that a junior member of the office staff has a satellite dish.
Ill Met by Moonlight: Suburban sitcom. When their local pub landlord is advised to take a rest by his doctor, Terry and June offer to run the place for him. What could go wrong?
Mistaken Identity: Classic British suburban sitcom. Terry loves to be the centre of attention - until he discovers he bears an uncanny resemblance to a criminal!
Lover Come Back to Me: Suburban sitcom. Terry experiences pangs of jealousy when June arranges to meet a childhood sweetheart. Advice from Sir Dennis compounds the situation.
Pantomania: More mirth with the Medfords. Terry and June play the cow in the office pantomime, but have trouble getting out of their costume when the hall is evacuated.
